documents.title: Getting Started
documents.content:
Installation



 Configuration

 1. TypeScript Configuration

Recommended: Use the @wsxjs/wsx-tsconfig package includes all required configurations:



Manual Configuration if not using @wsxjs/wsx-tsconfig:



> ⚠️ Important: If using the @state decorator, you must configure:
> - experimentalDecorators: true - Enable decorator syntax
> - useDefineForClassFields: false - Ensure decorators are compatible with class fields
>
> Additionally, you must configure @wsxjs/wsx-vite-plugin in vite.config.t

documents.title: GoPDF.js Design Documentation
documents.content:
I. Framework Introduction

GoPDF.js is a modern Web Components framework that focuses on JSX syntax and TypeScript support, completely zero dependency on React, dedicated to the ultimate development experience of native WebComponent capabilities.

- Core Philosophy: WebComponent + JSX + TypeScript, ultimate type safety and development efficiency.
- wsx files: Web Component with JSX, using .wsx extension, equivalent to TSX syntax for WebComponent.
- Self-validation: The examples directory is the

documents.title: WebComponent Guide
documents.content:
Overview

WebComponent is the standard custom element base class provided by GoPDF.js, using Shadow DOM to provide complete style isolation and encapsulation. It is the preferred choice for building reusable UI components, especially suitable for scenarios requiring style isolation and complete encapsulation.

 Why Use WebComponent?

 Use Cases

1. Reusable UI Components
   - Common components like buttons, inputs, cards, etc.
   - Components requiring complete style isolation
   - Component lib

documents.title: LightComponent Usage Guide
documents.content:
Overview

LightComponent is a lightweight custom element base class provided by GoPDF.js, designed for scenarios that require integration with third-party libraries or use Light DOM. It directly inherits from HTMLElement, does not use Shadow DOM, and provides complete reactive state management and JSX support.

 Why Use LightComponent?

 Use Cases

1. Third-party Library Integration
   - Integration with libraries like EditorJS, Chart.js, etc.
   - Libraries need direct access to DOM elements

documents.title: GoPDF.js JSX Support Documentation
documents.content:
Overview

GoPDF.js provides complete JSX support without relying on React or other frameworks. Through framework-level configuration, any project using WSX can get out-of-the-box JSX experience.

 Core Features

- ✅ Zero React Dependency: Completely independent JSX implementation
- ✅ Framework-level Support: No additional configuration needed in consumer projects
- ✅ TypeScript Native Support: Complies with TypeScript standard mechanisms
- ✅ IDE Friendly: Complete type hints and error checking
-

documents.title: GoPDF.js Design Philosophy
documents.content:
Overview

GoPDF.js is a native-first Web Components framework dedicated to providing the closest experience to native Web development while maintaining the convenience of modern frameworks.

 Core Design Principles

 1. Native-First

GoPDF.js prioritizes native Web standards and APIs over creating abstraction layers.

 Example: class vs className



Design Rationale:
- class is the standard HTML attribute name
- Avoids JavaScript reserved word conflicts
- More aligned with native Web development

documents.content:

> Best Practice: This publishing workflow follows best practices for open-source npm package publishing, including security checks, dry-run testing, version verification, etc.

 Prerequisites

1. Install Turbo already included in devDependencies
   

2. Ensure logged into NPM
   

3. Ensure on main branch and all changes committed

documents.title: Example Component Development Plan
documents.content:
WSX Example Component Development Plan

 Goals

Fully demonstrate GoPDF.js capabilities through rich example components, providing developers with best practice references.

 Development Principles

1. Teaching First - Each example should clearly demonstrate specific functionality
2. Code Simplicity - Avoid over-complexity, focus on core concepts
3. Practical - Provide reusable patterns and solutions
4. Well Documented - Each example should have detailed comments

 Phase 1: Core Feature Examples

documents.title: DOM Cache and Key Best Practices
documents.content:
Overview

GoPDF.js uses DOM caching mechanism to optimize rendering performance and avoid unnecessary DOM element reconstruction. Understanding how to correctly use the key attribute is crucial for ensuring application correctness and performance.

 Core Concepts

 What is DOM Cache?

DOM cache is a performance optimization technique that:
- Reuses existing DOM elements instead of destroying and rebuilding
- Only updates changed attributes and child elements
- Identifies and matches elements thr
